---
title: "Alluvial Plot"
author: "Elad Deiss-Yehiely"
date: "2026-02-13"
output: html_document
---
```{r}
sam_complete <- sam_sub %>%
  group_by(Subject_ID) %>%
  filter(all(c(0, 4, 12) %in% Age)) %>%
  ungroup()

genus_levels <- c("Moraxella","Streptococcus","Staphylococcus",
                  "Dolosigranulum","Corynebacterium","Haemophilus","Other")

sam_wide <- sam_complete %>%
  mutate(
    Age = as.character(Age),
    domgen_sum = factor(trimws(domgen_sum), levels = genus_levels)
  ) %>%
  select(Subject_ID, clust_sorensen_2, Age, domgen_sum) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = Age, values_from = domgen_sum) %>%
  rename(age0 = `0`, age4 = `4`, age12 = `12`) %>%
  mutate(
    age0  = factor(age0,  levels = genus_levels),
    age4  = factor(age4,  levels = genus_levels),
    age12 = factor(age12, levels = genus_levels)
  )

paths <- sam_wide %>%
  count(clust_sorensen_2, age0, age4, age12, name = "n") %>%
  group_by(clust_sorensen_2) %>%
  mutate(pct = n / sum(n)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(alluvium = row_number())

lodes <- ggalluvial::to_lodes_form(paths, axes = c("age0","age4","age12"), id = "alluvium")

axis_col <- intersect(names(lodes), c("key","x","axis"))[1]

# Go back to reversed factor levels
lodes_fixed <- lodes %>%
  rename(x_axis = all_of(axis_col)) %>%
  mutate(
    x_axis  = factor(as.character(x_axis), levels = c("age0","age4","age12")),
    stratum = factor(as.character(stratum), levels = rev(genus_levels))  # Reversed!
  ) %>%
  group_by(clust_sorensen_2, x_axis) %>%
  mutate(pct_recalc = n / sum(n)) %>%
  ungroup()

ggplot(
  lodes_fixed,
  aes(
    x = x_axis,
    stratum = stratum,
    alluvium = alluvium,
    y = pct_recalc,
    fill = stratum
  )
) +
  geom_flow(stat = "alluvium", alpha = 0.8, decreasing = TRUE) +
  geom_stratum(decreasing = TRUE) +
  facet_wrap(
    ~ clust_sorensen_2,
    nrow = 1,
    labeller = labeller(clust_sorensen_2 = c(
      "1-1" = "<span style='color:#006400;'>Trajectory 1</span>",
      "1-2" = "<span style='color:#D41159;'>Trajectory 2</span>"
    ))
  ) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c(age0 = "0", age4 = "4", age12 = "12")) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1),
    limits = c(0, 1),
    expand = c(0, 0)
  ) +
  scale_fill_discrete(limits = genus_levels, breaks = genus_levels, drop = FALSE) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(x = "Age (months)", y = "Percent within trajectory") +
  theme(
    legend.title = element_blank(),

    # 🔹 Larger axis text
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 18),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 18),
    axis.text.x  = element_text(size = 15),
    axis.text.y  = element_text(size = 15),

    # 🔹 Enable colored facet labels
    strip.text = ggtext::element_markdown(size = 18, face = "bold")
  )

```


### Making a chi-squared table for chronic rhinitis and trajectory membership ###
```{r}
library(dplyr)

make_participant_df <- function(df, outcome) {
  df %>%
    group_by(Subject_ID) %>%
    #filter(all(c(0, 4, 12) %in% Age)) %>%          # keep complete trajectory (optional)
    summarise(
      clust_sorensen_2 = dplyr::first(clust_sorensen_2),
      outcome_value    = dplyr::first(.data[[outcome]]),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    filter(!is.na(clust_sorensen_2), !is.na(outcome_value)) %>%
    mutate(
      clust_sorensen_2 = factor(clust_sorensen_2),
      outcome_value    = factor(outcome_value)
    )
}

chi_for_outcome <- function(df_long, outcome) {
  d <- make_participant_df(df_long, outcome)
  tab <- table(d$clust_sorensen_2, d$outcome_value)

  # Use Fisher automatically if expected counts are small
  chi <- suppressWarnings(chisq.test(tab))
  use_fisher <- any(chi$expected < 5)

  p <- if (use_fisher) fisher.test(tab)$p.value else chi$p.value

  list(
    outcome = outcome,
    n = nrow(d),
    table = tab,
    test = if (use_fisher) "Fisher" else "Chi-squared",
    p_value = p
  )
}

outs <- c("chronic_rhinitis12m", "chronic_rhinitis24m_current", "chronic_rhinitis36m_current")

res <- lapply(outs, \(o) chi_for_outcome(sam_sub_age4, o))

summary_tbl <- data.frame(
  outcome = sapply(res, `[[`, "outcome"),
  n       = sapply(res, `[[`, "n"),
  test    = sapply(res, `[[`, "test"),
  p_value = sapply(res, `[[`, "p_value")
) #%>%
  #mutate(q_value = p.adjust(p_value, method = "fdr")
         

summary_tbl



library(dplyr)
library(stringr)
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)

# --- Make participant-level data (1 row per Subject_ID) ---
make_participant_df <- function(df, outcome, require_ages = NULL) {
  df2 <- df %>% mutate(Age = as.character(Age))

  # Optional: require complete longitudinal ages (ONLY for full sam_sub, not sam_sub_age4)
  if (!is.null(require_ages)) {
    req <- as.character(require_ages)
    df2 <- df2 %>%
      group_by(Subject_ID) %>%
      filter(all(req %in% Age)) %>%
      ungroup()
  }

  df2 %>%
    group_by(Subject_ID) %>%
    summarise(
      Trajectory = dplyr::first(clust_sorensen_2),
      Outcome    = dplyr::first(.data[[outcome]]),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    filter(!is.na(Trajectory), !is.na(Outcome))
}

# --- Standardize outcome to Yes/No (robust to TRUE/FALSE, 0/1, "Yes"/"No") ---
to_yes_no <- function(x) {
  x_chr <- as.character(x)
  x_chr <- trimws(tolower(x_chr))

  yes_vals <- c("1","true","t","yes","y","case","positive")
  no_vals  <- c("0","false","f","no","n","control","negative")

  out <- ifelse(x_chr %in% yes_vals, "Yes",
                ifelse(x_chr %in% no_vals, "No", NA_character_))
  factor(out, levels = c("No","Yes"))
}

# --- Run test + return one row summary with counts embedded ---
one_outcome_row <- function(df, outcome, require_ages = NULL) {
  d <- make_participant_df(df, outcome, require_ages) %>%
    mutate(
      Trajectory = factor(Trajectory),
      Outcome = to_yes_no(Outcome)
    ) %>%
    filter(!is.na(Outcome))

  # Counts per trajectory
  counts <- d %>%
    count(Trajectory, Outcome) %>%
    tidyr::complete(Trajectory, Outcome, fill = list(n = 0)) %>%
    group_by(Trajectory) %>%
    mutate(N_traj = sum(n)) %>%
    ungroup()

  # If any trajectory has 0 participants after filtering, flag it
  if (nrow(counts) == 0 || any(counts$N_traj == 0)) {
    return(tibble(
      Outcome = outcome,
      `N (T1)` = NA_integer_,
      `Yes (T1)` = NA_character_,
      `No (T1)`  = NA_character_,
      `N (T2)` = NA_integer_,
      `Yes (T2)` = NA_character_,
      `No (T2)`  = NA_character_,
      Test = NA_character_,
      `p-value` = NA_real_,
      Note = "No data after filtering"
    ))
  }

  # Build 2x2 table
  tab <- table(d$Trajectory, d$Outcome)

  # Need at least 2 trajectory levels and 2 outcome levels
  if (nrow(tab) < 2 || ncol(tab) < 2) {
    # Still report counts
    # Grab first two trajectory names if exist
    trajs <- levels(d$Trajectory)
    traj1 <- trajs[1]
    traj2 <- ifelse(length(trajs) >= 2, trajs[2], NA)

    fmt <- function(traj, val) {
      n <- counts %>% filter(Trajectory == traj, Outcome == val) %>% pull(n)
      N <- counts %>% filter(Trajectory == traj) %>% slice(1) %>% pull(N_traj)
      paste0(n, " (", round(100 * n / N, 1), "%)")
    }

    return(tibble(
      Outcome = outcome,
      `N (T1)` = counts %>% filter(Trajectory == traj1) %>% slice(1) %>% pull(N_traj),
      `Yes (T1)` = fmt(traj1, "Yes"),
      `No (T1)`  = fmt(traj1, "No"),
      `N (T2)` = if (!is.na(traj2)) counts %>% filter(Trajectory == traj2) %>% slice(1) %>% pull(N_traj) else NA_integer_,
      `Yes (T2)` = if (!is.na(traj2)) fmt(traj2, "Yes") else NA_character_,
      `No (T2)`  = if (!is.na(traj2)) fmt(traj2, "No")  else NA_character_,
      Test = NA_character_,
      `p-value` = NA_real_,
      Note = "Not enough levels for a test"
    ))
  }

  # Choose chi-square vs fisher
  chi <- suppressWarnings(chisq.test(tab))
  use_fisher <- any(chi$expected < 5)
  p <- if (use_fisher) fisher.test(tab)$p.value else chi$p.value
  test_name <- if (use_fisher) "Fisher" else "Chi-squared"

  # Helper to format n (%)
  fmt <- function(traj, val) {
    n <- counts %>% filter(Trajectory == traj, Outcome == val) %>% pull(n)
    N <- counts %>% filter(Trajectory == traj) %>% slice(1) %>% pull(N_traj)
    paste0(n, " (", round(100 * n / N, 1), "%)")
  }

  trajs <- rownames(tab)
  traj1 <- trajs[1]
  traj2 <- trajs[2]

  tibble(
    Outcome = outcome,
    `N (T1)` = counts %>% filter(Trajectory == traj1) %>% slice(1) %>% pull(N_traj),
    `Yes (T1)` = fmt(traj1, "Yes"),
    `No (T1)`  = fmt(traj1, "No"),
    `N (T2)` = counts %>% filter(Trajectory == traj2) %>% slice(1) %>% pull(N_traj),
    `Yes (T2)` = fmt(traj2, "Yes"),
    `No (T2)`  = fmt(traj2, "No"),
    Test = test_name,
    `p-value` = p,
    Note = NA_character_
  )
}

# --- Main wrapper: produce kable ---
make_chi_kable <- function(df, outcomes, require_ages = NULL,
                           traj_labels = c("Trajectory 1", "Trajectory 2")) {

  # Function to assign stars
  p_stars <- function(p) {
    if (is.na(p)) return(NA)
    if (p <= 0.001) return("***")
    if (p <= 0.01)  return("**")
    if (p <= 0.05)  return("*")
    return("")
  }

  out_tbl <- dplyr::bind_rows(
    lapply(outcomes, \(o) one_outcome_row(df, o, require_ages))
  )

  out_tbl <- out_tbl %>%
    mutate(
      Outcome = Outcome %>%
        stringr::str_remove("_current$") %>%
        stringr::str_replace_all("_", " ") %>%
        stringr::str_replace_all("\\b(\\d+)m\\b", "\\1 mo") %>%
        stringr::str_to_title(),

      p_formatted = ifelse(
        is.na(`p-value`),
        NA,
        {
          stars <- sapply(`p-value`, p_stars)
          p_val <- signif(`p-value`, 3)
          formatted <- paste0(p_val, stars)
          ifelse(`p-value` < 0.05,
                 paste0("<b>", formatted, "</b>"),
                 formatted)
        }
      )
    )

  out_tbl_clean <- out_tbl %>%
    transmute(
      Outcome,
      `T1_Yes` = `Yes (T1)`,
      `T1_No`  = `No (T1)`,
      `T2_Yes` = `Yes (T2)`,
      `T2_No`  = `No (T2)`,
      `p-value` = p_formatted
    )

  # Create colored header labels (HTML styling)
  header_labels <- c(
    "Outcome",
    paste0("<span style='color:#006400;'>", traj_labels[1], "</span> (Yes)"),
    paste0("<span style='color:#006400;'>", traj_labels[1], "</span> (No)"),
    paste0("<span style='color:#D41159;'>", traj_labels[2], "</span> (Yes)"),
    paste0("<span style='color:#D41159;'>", traj_labels[2], "</span> (No)"),
    "p-value"
  )

  colnames(out_tbl_clean) <- header_labels

  out_tbl_clean %>%
    knitr::kable(format = "html", escape = FALSE, align = "lccccc") %>%
    kableExtra::kable_styling(
      full_width = FALSE,
      bootstrap_options = c("striped", "condensed")
    ) %>%
    kableExtra::row_spec(0, bold = TRUE)
}



make_chi_kable(
  df = sam_sub_age4,
  outcomes = outs,
  require_ages = NULL,
  traj_labels = c("1-1" = "Trajectory 1", "1-2" = "Trajectory 2")
)



library(webshot2)
library(htmltools)
library(kableExtra)

# 1️⃣ Create base table object
tbl <- make_chi_kable(
  df = sam_sub,
  outcomes = outs,
  require_ages = NULL,
  traj_labels = c("1-1" = "Trajectory 1",
                  "1-2" = "Trajectory 2")
)

# 2️⃣ Apply styling (IMPORTANT: save this object)
tbl_styled <- tbl %>%
  kableExtra::kable_styling(
    full_width = FALSE,
    bootstrap_options = c("striped", "condensed"),
    font_size = 22   # Increase for larger font (20–26 good range)
  ) %>%
  kableExtra::row_spec(0, bold = TRUE)

# 3️⃣ Save as HTML
htmltools::save_html(
  htmltools::tagList(tbl_styled),
  "~/Desktop/chi_trajectory_table.html"
)

# 4️⃣ Save as high-resolution PNG
webshot2::webshot(
  "~/Desktop/chi_trajectory_table.html",
  file = "~/Desktop/chi_trajectory_table.png",
  vwidth = 1600,
  vheight = 600,
  zoom = 2.5   # Increase for very crisp output
)

# Save as HTML first
kableExtra::save_kable(tbl, file = "chi_trajectory_table.html")

# Then save as an image (PNG) or PDF
kableExtra::save_kable(tbl, file = "chi_trajectory_table.png")
#kableExtra::save_kable(tbl, file = "chi_trajectory_table.pdf")








library(webshot2)
library(htmltools)
library(kableExtra)

# Build the table (this should already return an HTML table / kable object)
tbl <- make_chi_kable(
  df = sam_sub,
  outcomes = outs,
  require_ages = NULL,
  traj_labels = c("1-1" = "Trajectory 1",
                  "1-2" = "Trajectory 2")
)

# If you want additional styling, do it here (optional)
tbl_styled <- tbl %>%
  kableExtra::kable_styling(
    full_width = FALSE,
    bootstrap_options = c("striped", "condensed"),
    font_size = 22
  ) %>%
  kableExtra::row_spec(0, bold = TRUE)

# ✅ IMPORTANT: wrap in HTML() so tags are not escaped
page <- htmltools::tags$html(
  htmltools::tags<head>(
    htmltools::tags$meta(charset = "utf-8")
  ),
  htmltools::tags$body(
    htmltools::HTML(as.character(tbl_styled))
  )
)

html_file <- "~/Desktop/chi_trajectory_table.html"
png_file  <- "~/Desktop/chi_trajectory_table.png"

htmltools::save_html(page, file = html_file)

webshot2::webshot(
  html_file,
  file = png_file,
  vwidth = 1600,
  vheight = 600,
  zoom = 2.5
)








library(webshot2)
library(kableExtra)

# Create table
tbl <- make_chi_kable(
  df = sam_sub,
  outcomes = outs,
  require_ages = NULL,
  traj_labels = c("1-1" = "Trajectory 1",
                  "1-2" = "Trajectory 2")
)

# Style it
tbl_styled <- tbl %>%
  kableExtra::kable_styling(
    full_width = FALSE,
    bootstrap_options = c("striped", "condensed"),
    font_size = 22
  ) %>%
  kableExtra::row_spec(0, bold = TRUE)

# Save directly as PNG (no intermediate HTML needed)
kableExtra::save_kable(
  tbl_styled,
  file = "~/Desktop/chi_trajectory_table.png",
  zoom = 2.5
)

```
